codeReview.summary: The bottom tab navigation feature is well-implemented. The BottomTabNavigation component correctly renders 5 tabs with lucide-react icons, uses fixed positioning at the viewport bottom, highlights the active tab in blue (#0066FF), and navigates to the correct routes. The LayoutWrapper conditionally hides the tab bar on /login, /register, and /admin routes. Tests are comprehensive (11 tests covering all tab states). Manual verification via agent-browser confirmed correct behavior. A few non-blocking observations noted below.

issues.description: Icon choice mismatch: The feature description specifies 'football icon' and 'basketball icon', but the implementation uses Trophy (🏆) for Football and Dumbbell (💪) for Basketball. While lucide-react doesn't have exact football/basketball icons, a more intuitive mapping might be possible (e.g., 'Circle' for a ball shape, or 'Goal' if available). This is minor since the labels still clearly identify each tab.

The hideBottomTabs logic uses exact path matching for /login and /register but startsWith for /admin. This is inconsistent but functionally correct for current routes. If future auth routes are added under /login/* or /register/*, tabs would still show on those subroutes. Consider using startsWith for all excluded paths for consistency.

The UserLayout test is a placeholder that just asserts `expect(true).toBe(true)`. While the LayoutWrapper's conditional rendering was verified via agent-browser, there's no automated test for the LayoutWrapper component itself. A unit test could mock usePathname and verify that BottomTabNavigation is rendered or not based on route.

The LayoutWrapper wraps the entire app body in a client component ('use client'). This means the root layout effectively renders everything through a client boundary. While this works, it means all child Server Components will still be server-rendered (React handles this correctly), but it's worth noting this pattern - a more targeted approach could use Next.js route groups (e.g., (user) and (auth) groups) to avoid the client wrapper at the root level entirely.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker modified the home page (app/page.tsx) content from the original scaffolding text to 'Goods / Product catalog coming soon...' as part of the tab layout feature. While sensible, this changes the existing page content beyond what's strictly needed for bottom tabs. Future features targeting the Goods home page may need to be aware the page structure was already modified here.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: app/page.tsx diff: changed from 'WCStubHub Clone - SAE-A Trading' heading with p-8 to 'Goods' heading with p-4 pb-20

sharedStateObservations.observation: All user-facing pages use pb-20 padding-bottom to prevent content from being hidden behind the fixed tab bar. This is a crucial convention for any future pages but is not explicitly documented in AGENTS.md. Any new user-facing page must include pb-20 or equivalent bottom padding.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: All 5 page files (app/page.tsx, app/football/page.tsx, app/basketball/page.tsx, app/concert/page.tsx, app/my/page.tsx) use className='min-h-screen p-4 pb-20'

sharedStateObservations.observation: The package.json dependencies were re-sorted alphabetically during this commit. This is fine but indicates the worker used a tool or command that auto-sorted the keys. Future workers should be aware dependencies are alphabetically sorted.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: package.json diff shows dependencies reordered from insertion-order to alphabetical

summary: PASS. The bottom-tab-layout feature is correctly implemented with all expected behaviors covered: 5-tab fixed bottom navigation with lucide-react icons, active tab highlighted in blue (#0066FF), conditional rendering that hides tabs on auth/admin pages, and proper route navigation. Tests are comprehensive with 11 cases covering all tab states. Manual verification confirmed correct behavior. Four non-blocking issues noted: (1) icon choices for Football/Basketball could be more intuitive, (2) inconsistent path matching strategy in LayoutWrapper, (3) placeholder test for UserLayout, (4) client component wrapper at root level. One key convention observation: all user pages need pb-20 bottom padding to avoid tab bar overlap, which should be documented in AGENTS.md.
